channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: OpenAI co-founder Andrej Karpathy joins Anthropic’s pre-training team
item.description: Andrej Karpathy has joined Anthropic to work on pre-training. He previously co-founded and worked at OpenAI and led computer vision and AI at Tesla.

item.title: Start Here: Why AI-Assisted Development Needs a Governance Layer
item.description: Every major shift in software engineering produces a new infrastructure requirement. The cloud era gave us orchestration. The DevOps era gave us CI/CD. The generative AI era is producing its own...

item.title: The Private Piggy Bank: A Beginner's Guide to Confidential Smart Contracts with CoFHE
item.description: Before We Begin: What Problem Are We Solving? Imagine you have a regular piggy bank sitting on your desk. You know how much money is inside. Nobody else does, because they cannot see through the...

item.title: Day 7 — funnel audit found 7 of 9 articles had no buy link, then I pivoted the product
item.description: Day 6: I dropped the $9 hard wall to pay-what-you-want from $1 and opened the affiliate program at 30%. Day 7 promise: post the numbers. Here they are. Then the thing I missed. Raw 24h delta...

item.title: 24 Hours After Opening: The First Inquiries, and the First Sale I Already Lost
item.description: Originally published at moday.me. Building MODAY in public. 24 Hours After Opening: The First Inquiries, and the First Sale I Already Lost Opened on 5/18. Two emails on 5/19. I opened the store on...

item.title: How I Built a Drone-Based Crack Detection Pipeline on AWS
item.description: I needed to build a pipeline that takes drone footage of infrastructure (bridges, facades, roads), detects surface defects like cracks and corrosion, and delivers actionable reports to engineers...

item.title: Why a multi-agent protocol that only enables note-passing leaves most of the value on the table.
item.description: When people set up two AI agents to work on the same project, the first instinct is to give them a shared file. A CLAUDE.md. An AGENTS.md. A Notion page. Now they can leave each other notes. They...

item.title: Stop Outsourcing Compute: Why the Browser is Your Best IDE
item.description: If you step back and look at the architecture of a standard web IDE today, it looks remarkably similar to the thin-client setups of the 1990s. We sit in front of machines with gigabytes of RAM,...

item.title: What's New: Terraform Support, Kubernetes and AWS Automation, Bring Your Own Bucket, and UX Improvements
item.description: What's New in OpenObserve: Terraform Support, Kubernetes and AWS Automation, Bring Your Own Bucket, and UX Improvements OpenObserve has shipped three major updates that help engineering teams...

item.title: Sovereign Synapse: The Great Export
item.description: For years, we have treated LLMs as a rented brain. We have poured our debugging sessions, research threads, and early project drafts into cloud-hosted chat windows, treating them as convenient...

item.title: SeekYou: How I Built a Unified Host Intelligence Tool That Runs Free on Cloudflare Workers
item.description: One input. 15 sources. One structured report. Zero infrastructure costs. The Problem Every security engineer knows the workflow. You get an IP, a domain, or an ASN you need to investigate. You...

item.title: Former OpenAI Staffers Warn That xAI’s Poor Safety Record Could Complicate SpaceX’s IPO
item.description: The ex-employees, who cofounded a new AI watchdog group, say investors deserve more information about xAI’s safety practices before SpaceX goes public.

item.title: America’s dangerous, messy deepfakes crackdown is here
item.description: A law requiring social networks to quickly remove sexual deepfakes and other nonconsensual imagery is now fully in force. But experts warn the policy could do little to help victims - and at worst...

item.title: Apple’s accessibility features add more AI-powered processing
item.description: Apple's annual update about accessibility features on its platforms has new additions for the iPhone, Mac, and especially Vision Pro, with a focus on how on-device AI processing is being used in...

item.title: DirtyDecrypt PoC Released for Linux Kernel CVE-2026-31635 LPE Vulnerability
item.description: Proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit code has now been released for a recently patched security flaw in the Linux kernel that could allow for local privilege escalation (LPE). Dubbed DirtyDecrypt (aka...

item.title: Anker fixes the two worst things about power stations
item.description: Anker's Solix S2000 solves two major pain points with power stations: idle power draw that can leave a battery unexpectedly dead, and the large footprint required to hold so much energy....

item.title: Understanding the modern cybercrime landscape
item.description: Throughout 2025, HPE observed significant changes in how cybercriminals operate. Analyzing real-world threats, our HPE Threat Labs highlighted an industrialization of the cyber criminals’ methods...

item.title: Nintendo keeps finding new ways to reinvent platformers
item.description: In most platforming games, you're fighting against the world around you. You're trying to beat a level, nail a seemingly impossible series of jumps, or defeat a powerful boss. But even though...

item.title: Microsoft launches Surface Pro 12 and Surface Laptop 8 with Intel chips
item.description: Almost exactly two years ago, Microsoft introduced Arm-powered versions of the Surface Pro 11 and Surface Laptop 7, with Intel models not ready until more than six months later. This time around...

item.title: The Mandalorian and Grogu should have been a season of TV
item.description: When The Mandalorian first debuted on Disney Plus, it was a refreshing reminder of how fascinating Star Wars stories can be when they aren't focused on the same handful of well-established...

item.title: The Download: Musk v. Altman, smart glasses for warfare, and Google I/O
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Here’s why Elon Musk lost his suit against OpenAI Elon...

item.title: Colossal Biosciences is growing chickens in a 3D-printed artificial eggshell
item.description: The baby chicks were shifting and starting to pip—or trying to hatch. But not from an egg. Instead, these chickens were growing inside transparent 3D-printed plastic cups at the Dallas...

item.title: Marshall brings ANC back to its smaller on-ear wireless headphones
item.description: Marshall's first headphones with active noise canceling were the Marshall Mid A.N.C. first launched in 2018. Its current lineup only offers noise canceling on its larger over-ear Monitor III...

item.title: These are the robot vacuum-mops I recommend for every type of home
item.description: Inviting a robot vacuum-mop into your life is a big decision. An autonomous floor-cleaning machine roaming your home is equal parts glorious and intrusive. But if it does its job well, you may...

item.title: Xbox fans want exclusives, more backward compatibility, and free online multiplayer
item.description: Microsoft launched a new Xbox Player Voice portal yesterday, aiming to collect feedback from fans and "make it more visible." It certainly hasn't taken long for Xbox fans to make their feedback...

item.title: The New Phishing Click: How OAuth Consent Bypasses MFA
item.description: In February 2026, a phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform called EvilTokens went live. Within five weeks, it had compromised more than 340 Microsoft 365 organizations across five countries. The...

item.title: Laurie Anderson Is Quoting Me
item.description: Not by name, but Laurie Anderson quotes me in one of the tracks of her new album: My favorite quote is from a cryptologist who said “If you think technology will solve your problems, you don’t...

item.title: Google I/O 2026: How to watch and what to expect
item.description: Google's annual developer conference has arrived. We're expecting plenty of updates to Gemini, Search, and every other product that Google has stuffed AI inside of. The keynote kicks off later...

item.title: Drupal to Release Urgent Core Security Updates on May 20, Sites Told to Prepare
item.description: Drupal has issued an alert stating that it intends to release a "core security release" for all supported branches on May 20, 2026, from 5-9 p.m. UTC. "The Drupal Security Team urges you to...

item.title: Tom Steyer Wants to Save California From Billionaires. But Also Doesn’t Want Them to Leave
item.description: The hedge fund billionaire turned gubernatorial candidate wants to tax California’s ultrawealthy, regulate AI, and keep Silicon Valley happy at the same time. Good luck with that.
